rationale: In the Study 4b conceptual setup, the authors explicitly cite Thomas and Morwitz (2009b) to justify a key mechanism: ease of computation influences perceived costs and discount judgments. This citation is tied to an implemented manipulation (different denominations) and to predicted effects on double mental discounting, not just a generic literature list. Although cited alongside another reference, it meaningfully supports hypothesis development and interpretation for one experimental test.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Thomas and Morwitz (2009b) as prior evidence that computational ease shapes perceived price magnitude. It then applies that idea to promotional-credit framing, predicting and testing that harder computations (points vs dollars) weaken mental discounting.
